text:
TOP 10 AI TOOLS RANKED IN 2026.

Each one makes every other tool you pay for look slow. Save this.

1. Claude
The best AI for writing, reasoning, and deep analysis. Reads entire codebases, research papers, books, and legal documents in one context window of up to 200,000 tokens. Produces the most nuanced long-form output of any model and leads independent benchmarks on instruction-following. Used by Cursor and Windsurf as the engine behind AI coding. Anthropic's published safety research is the most transparent in the industry.
Best for: writing, analysis, coding, research, long documents.
https://t.co/JTa800zJof

2. OpenClaw
An open-source autonomous AI agent built by Austrian developer Peter Steinberger. Launched in November 2025 under the name Clawdbot and surpassed 100,000 GitHub stars within 90 days. Does not respond to questions. It executes tasks. Runs on your local machine, connects to WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, and Discord, and carries out multi-step actions: sending emails, running scripts, managing files, booking reservations. Uses Claude and GPT-4 as its underlying models. Free and MIT licensed. Bring your own API key.
Best for: autonomous task execution, workflow automation, agentic AI.
https://t.co/26jlYHTQ49

3. Manus
China's first general AI agent, built by https://t.co/AjAvfKc2CL and released in March 2025. Operates independently across the web: browses sites, writes and executes code, manages files, fills forms, and delivers completed work rather than instructions. Achieved 500,000 waitlist signups in its first week. Runs a persistent virtual environment that continues working even when you close the browser. The first widely-used agent to complete end-to-end professional tasks without human input at each step.
Best for: autonomous research, multi-step web tasks, report generation.
https://t.co/kk9fNyWBWq

4. Wispr Flow
AI-powered voice dictation built by Wispr AI, founded in 2021 in San Francisco. Backed by $81 million in funding including a $30 million Series A from Menlo Ventures. Works system-wide across every app on Mac, Windows, iOS, and Android. Hold a hotkey, speak, and polished text appears wherever your cursor sits. Removes filler words, adds punctuation, and formats output for the app in context automatically. Transcribes at roughly 220 words per minute versus 45 words per minute typing. Free tier available. Pro is $15 per month.
Best for: dictation, voice-to-text, writing faster across any app.
https://t.co/zznAACtwDS

5. Nano Banana
Google's AI image generator and editor built on the Gemini Flash architecture. Launched in August 2025 and attracted 13 million first-time users to the Gemini app within weeks, particularly in emerging markets. Generates and edits images from plain-language prompts. The key difference from earlier generators is editing, not just generation: it modifies specific elements of existing images, maintains character consistency across iterations, and handles multi-step edits without regenerating the full image. Nano Banana 2 released February 2026 and is now the default image model across Gemini, Google Search, and Google AI Studio. Free on all Gemini plans.
Best for: AI image generation, photo editing, visual content creation.
https://t.co/o4V03hYtX7

6. Lovable
AI full-stack app builder that generates complete, production-ready web applications from a plain-language description. No code required. Raised $200 million at a $1.8 billion valuation in July 2025 and closed a $330 million Series B at a $6.6 billion valuation in December 2025 with $200 million ARR. Enterprise customers include Klarna, Uber, and Zendesk. Generates React and TypeScript, syncs with GitHub, integrates Supabase for backend and authentication, and deploys to Vercel. You own the code and can hand it to a developer at any point. Free tier available. Pro starts at $25 per month.
Best for: building apps, MVPs, SaaS products, internal tools without coding.
https://t.co/FzWgZqR8o9

7. Perplexity
AI-powered search engine that retrieves current information from the web and synthesizes it into a cited, readable answer. No blue links to click through. No SEO spam to filter. Every answer shows its sources inline so you can verify claims directly. Pro Search runs deeper multi-step queries across multiple sources before synthesizing. Used by researchers, journalists, and analysts who need accurate, up-to-date information fast. Over 15 million monthly active users as of 2025. Free tier available. Pro is $20 per month.
Best for: research, fact-checking, current events, cited answers.
https://t.co/jvZfmcPlQp

8. NotebookLM
Google's AI research assistant built specifically for your own documents. Upload PDFs, Google Docs, YouTube videos, audio files, and web pages. NotebookLM reads only your sources and answers questions grounded in them, with citations pointing to the exact passage. Does not hallucinate from general knowledge. Its Audio Overview feature converts any set of documents into a natural two-host podcast discussion in minutes. Used by researchers, students, lawyers, and journalists who need to work deeply with large document sets. Free for personal use via Google account.
Best for: document research, summarizing sources, study, podcast generation from documents.
https://t.co/TgG34ETkAS

9. ChatGPT
OpenAI's flagship AI assistant with over 500 million weekly active users as of early 2026. The most widely adopted AI product in history. GPT-4o handles text, images, audio, and code. Memory retains context across conversations over time. Custom GPTs allow users to build specialized versions for specific tasks. Deep Research mode performs autonomous multi-step web research and delivers structured reports. Operator integrations let ChatGPT take actions inside third-party apps. Canvas mode provides a collaborative document and code editor. Free tier available. Plus is $20 per month.
Best for: general-purpose AI assistance, coding, image generation, research, automation.
https://t.co/aVxw7NdvuL

10. Notion AI
AI layer built directly into Notion, the workspace used by over 100 million users. Writes, edits, summarizes, and translates inside any Notion page. Q&A feature searches your entire workspace and returns answers grounded in your own documents, meeting notes, and project pages. Autofill populates database properties automatically from existing content. Works across personal notes, team wikis, and company knowledge bases without switching apps or copying text. Free for limited use inside existing Notion plans. Full AI access is $10 per member per month added to any paid plan.
Best for: knowledge management, team wikis, document writing, workspace search.
https://t.co/8WCy2XRfu3

Decided to go to DC next week to talk directly with policymakers. Not sure how impactful it will be but with everything happening, feels like a good time to share more about open-source AI, transparency, concentration of power, the real risks vs the real benefits. Who do you think I should meet there (Congress members, WH people, public orgs,...)?

In order to see if the gov response was predictable, I pasted the wiki page about the Anthropic/DoD dispute into ChatGPT Pro, & told it Anthropic had released a model where it restricted use because it may be too dangerous.

tldr: "almost tailor-made to trigger" the government. https://t.co/5vkvWzEFQZ

I disagree with this decision and I don't like it.

But also...

HOW DID ANTHROPIC NOT SEE THIS COMING‽

It is *the* obvious response to "this is too dangerous for anyone except us to use", since that relies on a premise ("we are uniquely good") that almost no-one agrees with.

Diffusion is differentiable. LLMs aren't.

So why is the diffusion community copying RL methods (GRPO etc.) from LLMs?

The native post-training for diffusion is gradient descent such as ReFL and LeapAlign. Paper: https://t.co/uoy9mCGJSv
